publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-11-18 00:18:02 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Troy L. Smith Collection of Hip Hop Party and Show Tapes

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to collection restricted until digitization is complete.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Approximately 300 Hip Hop party and show tapes collected by Troy L. Smith.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Troy L. Smith Collection of Hip Hop Party and Show Tapes, #8127. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: 2. Use of digitized audio visual materials is limited to on-site access only.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: All tape descriptions are written by Troy Smith and were taken from http://www.historicalhiphop.com/. Smith's original tape titles have been revised for clarity. Tape length has been removed from titles and added to the end of the tape's scope and content. Dates were also removed from titles and added to the dedicated date begin and end fields. Where necessarily, punctuation and capitalization was adjusted to provide consistency and clarify artist and venue name. "Side A/B" designations have also been adjusted for clarity.

did.unittitle: 1-Fantastic Five, Caz, Busy Bee, Whiz Kid, Treacherous Three, Spoonie Gee at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dota Rock does a rhyme about things that are "like". Dota Rock opens to headhunters and then passes off to the treach three and then they pass off to Spoonie Gee. 60 min.

did.unittitle: 2-Treacherous Three at Club Negrils (1980, Side A)/Afrika Bambaataa, Jazzy J, Cosmic force, Soul Sonic Force at T-Connection (1982, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Treach 3 doing routines with two mics Sunshine rocking with out a mic. 90 min.

did.unittitle: 3-Crash Crew, Soul Sonic Force, Cosmic Force, Whiz Kid at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Crash crew open up doing routines off 8th wonder and bounce rock skate. Then they do their famous record. Pow Wow does a nursery Rhyme. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 4-Fantastic Five, Cold Crush Four, Soul Sonic Force, Bam, Whiz Kid at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bam is playing a plate with Flash on the beat box and they kill it, Dotta-Rock saying a rhyme about an Amazon, Caz, JDL, K Gee testing the mics to some dope cuts check tape 66 for the rest of the CC4 show. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 5-Busy Bee, Kevin Kev, Dota Rock, Grandmaster Caz, DJ A.J. Theodore at Audubon Ballroom

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape start with Waterbed kev rocking off of catch a groove and changing words to songs like it's the joint and sugar hill 8th wonder on the other side kev, busy bee and Caz rock the house together. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 6-Treacherous Three 2nd Anniversary, Cold Crush Four at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Treacherous Three ask the Furious Five for a battle and Caz disses a heckler in the crowd with a rhyme just for hecklers. Caz also does a rhyme about getting caught in the bed with a girl name Yvette. 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: More from this recording can be found on tape 271 (tr-19533)

did.unittitle: 7-Cold Crush Four, Imperial Brothers at Hoe Ave. Boys' Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J.D.L. said a girl in the crowd is sewing her draws because she said "so" to Caz announcement. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 8-Cold Crush Four, Disco Enforcers at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JDL opens up to sequence record. side B opens to 8th wonder with the crew doing a routine and the crew is rocking to freedom. Caz does a Rhyme about being somebody's father Caz also rhymes to another one bites the dust saying he caught J.D.L. and K.G. smoking dust. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 9-Rappers Convention –Fantastic Five, Crash Crew, Force MCs, Cold Crush Four, L.A. Sunshine at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Crash crew doing a lot of talking before they start rocking especially about the mic Doug E. Fresh was using. They do High Powered rap and there second album live and they rock them. Force MCs rock the beat box, Stevie Dee's uncle Jessie opens for the force by clowning around. Cold Crush starts with Caz doing a routine from a Richard Pryor album, by stating "the white man own the place he just fronting your black ass off. And J.D.L. is no longer with his ex girl Yvonne Baker. It's DJ A.J. and his sister Pam's birthday. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 10-Ecstacy Garage All Stars: Grandmaster Caz, JDL, Whipper Whip, K-Gee, Ruby Dee, Theodore, Soul Sonic Force, Nice and Nasty Four at Ecstasy Garage Disco

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JDL is rhyming off of 8th wonder and there all freestyle. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 11-Funk Machine - Grandmaster Caz, Donald Dee, Kid Vicious, Pow Wow, MC Globe, Afrika Islam, Fantastic Five at 118 Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The funk machine is doing work especially Caz, the side with fantastic is distorted but gets better. I don't know the date. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 12-Charlie Chase, Furious Five at the Forrest Projects/Mercedes Ladies at Inner City Disco

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mellie Mel opening with a echo chamber to good times. 60 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: 14-Cold Crush Four Battle the Fantastic Five at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From the very beginning when Caz comes on with a bullhorn tells the fantastic to come out with there hands up to the very end when they are judge by the audience. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 15-Crash Crew, Cold Crush Four, Mean Machine, Spoonie Gee at Harlem World and unknown location in Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is the Whole show of the Crash Crew they are doing routines and all of their popular records at that time. Caz screams on a heckler in the crowd and says his man Roy Thomas is in the hospital with the herpes and when he gets out "he will be walking like he has a potato chip in his ass and he don't want to break it". Mean Machine perform there popular song as well as do there routines. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 16-Fantastic Five, Cold Crush Four, Treacherous Three at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A.D.s birthday, and Fantastic doing routines pertaining to Christmas. 60 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: CC4 do a routine off the record war.

did.unittitle: 18-Fearless Four, Cold Crush Four at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caz-cracking jokes about fags, coke and herpes. Peso is singing on one of the routines. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 19-Cold Crush Four at Ecstasy Garage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caz and J.D.L. start to "another one bites the dust" later Caz does a rhyme about being someone's father as well as other rhymes. J.D.L. does one about a hotel room and K.G. does a short one about being a private eye. A.D. shows up later and they do a short set of routines. After there show Greg Ski comes on to DJ and of course Caz is snapping on someone's uncle Mary. 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is the classic battle from beginning to almost the end. All of Moe Dee's part is there. 60 mins.

scopecontent.p: This is before Raheim became a part of the crew, also Flash is cutting a lot of records sounds also like he is cutting a record backwards (Space funk). 60 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: 24-DJ Breakout, DJ Barren, Funky4+1, DJ Whiz Kid, Harmonizing 4 at T-Connection

did.unittitle: 26-Cold Crush Four, Kurtis Blow-DJ Davey Dee at Skate Palace, New Years Eve 1983

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kurtis Blow Does his song daydreaming along with his routines. All the Cold Crush is there and there all on point doing there famous routines A.D. for moment speaks about him not using drugs. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 27-Cold Crush Four, Force MCs, Busy Bee at Printing H.S./Cold Crush Four, Zulu Nation at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Busy Bee is only on for a short time, Jazzy FIve do there thing with some good routines and play the beat box for a minute. The force MCs start out singing a song and then they do there thing with routines off F-troop, Gilligans Island, Brady bunch the Adams family and I love to teach the world to sing also rock the beat box. The Cold crush don't complete there session cause it then goes into another party with the Cold Crush but now there at the T-Connection and J.D.L. is rockin his hut and Jazzy J is cutting. 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dota Rock does his rhyme about having a child and why you shouldn't be gay, Caz cracking jokes, the four do a routine off let it whip. 60 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush do there routines on the other side Jazzy J is mixing records before the Crush come on. 60 mins.

scopecontent.p: L.A. Sunshine standing in for J.D.L. Mele Mel, Big Daddy Kane, Whipper Whip and Kevin Kev get on the mic, and Starsky is Djing. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 32-Busy Bee, Master Don and the Death Committee, and the Cold Crush Four, as well as L.A. Sunshine at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Busy Bee rocking and he didn't know till the last minute and Master Don and the crew tear it up. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 33-Treacherous Three in Bermuda (1984, Side A)/Side B: DJ Hollywood at Harlem World (1982, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Treach do there records Body Rock, At the Party, Whip it, We'll turn you on, Action, Get up, Feel the Heartbeat and a Jackson 5 routine then they freestyle off of the record sucker MC the breakers in attendance are Fastbreak and Mr. Wiggles. Side B Hollywood starts mixing and rocking to numbers and super freak he does "well" routine and his chorus in the back ground of MC Artie Art and Disco Romeo and sounds like there might be others. This is a Andre Harrell production. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 34-JDL, Almighty KG, Ruby Dee, Hi-C, Theodore, DJ Magic Mike, Quinny Queen at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 36-Grand Wizzard Theodore, Afrika Bambaataa, Rocksteady at Danceteria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 120 mins.

did.unittitle: 37-Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five, Kool Kyle, DJ Sinbad at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Raheim starts off rocking to Johnny the fox. Cowboy comes behind and is rocking off of the bells, later FLASH is cutting "listen to the base man" and the crowd is loving it also he gets on the beat box and the do their routine and its Kool Kyle's birthday and he is singing off of got to be real and Mel is saying some dope rhymes to the record groove to get down. This is a really good party they all rock. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 38-Cosmic Force, Soulsonic Force, Jazzy Jay at Amityville Long Island (undated, Side A)/Pow Wow, Raheim, Lisa Lee, Ice Ice, Sundance, Africa Islam, Jazzy Jay etc. at outside jam (undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: Bam rocks the beat box. Side B: Pow Wow makes a shout out to crazy phil Lisa Lee does her thing like I never heard before. They all rock the echo chamber. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 39-Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This the only routine I ever heard on there tapes other then them doing there records and it's the "till the break of dawn" routine. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 40-T-Ski Valley at Harlem World MC Throw Down

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: He not only does his record catch the beat, he also does other routines that are pretty good. Also Spoonie Gee, Busy Bee, Force MC, Fantastic Five, Cold Crush Four. Note: the quality of the sound is not the best, but with the EQ it sounds fine.

did.unittitle: 41-Cold Crush Four, Treacherous Three at Hoe Ave. Boys' Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: 42-DJ Star Child at the Love Nest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Star Child Starts with Susan Vegas Toms Diner. 90mins. Q10.

did.unittitle: 43-Cold Crush Four and Busy Bee at Harlem World (1981, Side A)/Grandmaster Flash and Kurtis Blow at unknown location (1979, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: Caz, J.DL and Busy Bee rocking Hut together. Caz says "to all the virgins in the house, thanks for nothing". Side B: on that same side Zulu nation Long Island with Mr. Big, Hutch Hutch, and Pow Wow and he is doing a rhyme about buffalo bill - date unknown. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 44-Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five at T-Connection 1980

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: Cowboy opens up to Got to be real and he is using an echo chamber. Side B: Force MC Broadway international 1983. Note: Bee Fats is cutting after the force finishes. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 45-Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five and the Fantastic Five at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five Mellie Mel opening up for the crew with cowboy following off of breaking bells and playing other records such as head hunters, Johnny the fox and rocket in the pocket. Fantastic Five. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 46-Cold Crush Four, Treacherous Three on boat ride coming from Bear Mountain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This party they are coming back from Bear Mountain and Caz says they are getting there $5.50 worth, Caz and JDL rocking Hut. Check tape 124 for them going to Bear Mountain. I finally found it! 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 47-The Incredible 3 MCs, Tech Ski, Destiny, Chucky Chuck and DJ Lil Chase at unknown location (Side A)/Funky Four + 1 at unknown location (circa 1979, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: No location or date and it appear to be three different shows by them.Side B: KK Rockwell starts by saying "he likes to do his thing" off the record got to be real and the other MCs one by one say the same thing one by one then they go in to routines. There is no date or location safe to assume 79.

did.unittitle: 48-Treacherous Three at unknown location (undated, Side A)/Kool Moe Dee at unknown location in Greenville, South Carolina (1987, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A:: This is a nice clear tape and the party is cool. Side B: Moe dee is doing his records wild wild west, how you like me now etc, also speaks about the feud between him and LL cool J at the Apollo. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 49-Cold Crush Four, Funky 4+1 - Halloween at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caz and JDL are doing there notorious two routine as well as there others with A.D. and K.G. Caz also says its not as pack as the night before when they were at south Bx. High school and the funky four are doing a lot of shout outs to the fantastic and cold crush as well as Busy Bee and then they do a few routines and some nice free style. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 50-Doug E. Fresh, Kid West, and others at different parties, a home recording, and Busy Bee and Rock steady in Tokyo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 51-Cold Crush Four, Fantastic Five, Boogie Boys at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 52-Funky Four at T-Connection, Master Don and the Death Committee at unknown location in Patterson N.J.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Funky Four are using an echo chamber. They sing a routine from a Gladys Knight song. And do their famous song funk box. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 53-Black History Month-Cold Crush Four, D. St. Infinity Rappers, Debbie Dee, Jazzy Five, Jazzy Jay, Magical Five, Busy Bee at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This tape Caz dedicates the next routine to the most supt. Up niggers that charge $7 and don't do a show, D.ST I believe is using a synthesizer, the jazzy five music went out cause somebody step on the cord and their was a fight. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 54-Force MCs battle Cold Crush Four at unknown location in Patterson, N.J.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This tape was cleaned up by my man Dagod9 only flaw it's a bit slow in the very beginning but after that its dynamite. The force put on an excellent show but the Cold Crush, from the words of Caz, never went there to battle, in fact the Force wasn't there when the Cold Crush was on stage, the Force was coming in when the Cold Crush was leaving. And if you listen close the Cold Crush never makes reference to the Force. But it still was a good show. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 55-Doug E. Fresh battles Master Don and the Death Committee at unknown location (undated, Side A)/Doug E. Fresh and the Get Fresh Crew, the Tranquilizing Three at unknown location (undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dougies human beat box battles Barry Bee's turntables. Also Barry Bee battles DJ Smalls. Master Don cuts with sneakers on his hands. And Master Dons MCs battle Doug saying he wears Corduroys in the summer but the tape doesn't have Doug battling them back. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 56-L-Brothers battle Herculoids at unknown location (1978, Side A)/L-brothers and the Zulu Nation at T-Connection (1979, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A sounds pretty scratchy needs work. Side B is the bomb, Theodore cuts super sperm better then any body in this world. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 57-Live Convention - L-Brothers, Treacherous Three, Busy Bee, Mellie Mel, Grandmaster Flash, Theodore, E-Man, Smiley at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is an amazing tape because this is were the 81, 82 convention albums were made from. its not the best quality tape, in fact I had to fill in some parts from the albums because it sounded so bad from hissing, but you can tell very easily that those albums were taken from this tape, and its really from 1980 and they are rocking at the celebrity club and it's Smiley's birthday! 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Second half of recording can be found on tape 132 (tr-19394)

did.unittitle: 58-Zulu Nation: Hutch Hutch, Mr. Big, Pow Wow, Sundance, Africa Islam at Bronx River Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One of the many jams they rock is "toys in the attic". 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 59-Zulu Nation: DJ Jazzy Jay, DJ Robbie Rob, MC Globe, Lil Ikey C, Pow Wow, Mr. Biggs at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Second record Bam cuts is Hut and later on "1,2,3,4 hit" it as well as "that's the joint" and bustin loose and the telephone jam, in that order. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 60-Zulu Nation: Charlie Rock, Bam, Red Alert, Whiz kid, Bill Blast, Pow Wow, MC Globe at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A little static, they rock a lot of songs such as love and effect ion, Numbers, "Can I shine yawl shoes", Funky penguin, big beat and others. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 61-Grandmaster Flash mix (unknown location)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flash cutting such records as Children growing, Toys in the attic, the honey drippers, Corzon, Dance to the drummers beat etc.

did.unittitle: 62-Grandmaster Caz break beats (unknown location)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caz cutting such records as jimmy castors "its just begun", Rock creek park, the Mexican, Frisco disco, Keep your distance, Mary Mary etc. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 63-DJ Hollywood, Eddie Cheeba, Starski, DJ Divine, Grandmaster Flash, Mellie Mel and Kurtis Blow at Jamaica Queens Armory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a good party and a fine tape of sound, it was well preserved. And some of the MCs make reference to the sugar hill gang in a negative way, also Flash rocks the beat box. 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Remainder of recording can be found on tape 183 (tr-19445) and tape 250 (tr-19512)

did.unittitle: 64-Treacherous Three and T La Rock at unknown location in Hempstead, Long Island (1984, Side A)/ Treacherous Three (1st anniversary), DJ Starski, Spoonie Gee and Busy Bee at Harlem World (1981, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: This side of the tape the Treach are truly rocking and T La Rock does his cut "It's yours" the quality is real good no hissing what so ever only problem it's only coming out of one speaker so I am working on trying to get it to come out of the other side. Side B: the party is dope but the quality gets kind of bad when the tape gets to the treach starting there show. Busy bee sounds real good at the end rocking off heartbeat but he goes off too soon, but I enjoyed the show.)90 mins.

did.unittitle: 65-Fantastic Five at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sounds like two different party's the first they make reference to it being Cool Herc and Bambaataa's birthday and the other makes reference to Mr. Magic in the house. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 66-Dota Rock's birthday party at T-Connection (1980, Side A)/Fantastic Five, Cold Crush Four, Soul Sonic Force, Bam, Whiz Kid at T-Connection (Side B, 1981)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is the second half of the party, I am looking for the first half. Also Fantastic announces they were going to battle the Crash crew the next night. Side B: This is the end of tape 4. The CC4 do there routines and Wiz kid is cutting in the place of Charlie Chase. Caz also Sings off of "ain't no stopping us now". 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recordings related to this can be found on Tape 103 (tr-19365), Tape 133 (tr-19395), and Tape 294 (tr-19556)

did.unittitle: 67-Fantastic Five first anniversary at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a radio facing the speaker tape and this is one of there better shows only problem like always it seems they have mic problems, this is a dam good show they put on there is a lot of crowd participation. any body got the straight to tape show please hit me with it. Also the Harlem World crew rocks in between some hissing but better then I thought. Don't sleep on this tape. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 68-Busy Bee, Fearless Four, Devastating 3, Sweet G. at unknown location in Queens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Darrell C is cutting for Busy Bee and Busy is a funny brother before he starts rocking talking about he lives across from abandon buildings in the Bronx also its his birthday. anybody got the straight to tape show. Radio to speaker. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 69-Mean Gene, Theodore at 3rd Ave. Ballroom

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Although this is a radio to speaker tape you would never know until the MC comes on. Theodore and his brother are cutting a lot of good records. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 70-Dr.Jeckyl and Mr. Hyde at unknown location (circa 1979, Side A)/Grandmaster Flash supposedly at The Disco Fever (1984, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Both sides sound kind of scratchy and hissing, the Jeckyl side which they open up to toys in the attic and Ronnie green is the DJ and Flash starts with his Jam "it was a party night".

did.unittitle: 71-Busy Bee (first anniversary), Furious Five, Cold Crush Four at P.A.L

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Charlie Chase is there cutting for busy bee. flash not there for the Furious Five so all they are doing is passing the mic. JDL is not there for the Cold crush which is kool cause Caz holds it down, doing his rhymes plus his song off of "ain't no stopping us" record. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 72-Busy Bee, Cold Crush Four, Fantastic Five at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Busy Bee starts off to Freedom and then says sneak preview as he starts to rock to funky president then JDL starts rocking with busy and Caz gets on with them to Hut, also rocking "On" as well as Mary Mary. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 73-Master Don and the Death Committee, Force MCs, Doug E. Fresh and the Crash crew at Savoy Manor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Master Don and the crew do work in this show. Not only do they do routine off of Gladys knight theme they also do one off of Sly and the family stone as well as others and Don rocks the beat box. Doug E. Fresh does his thing, also disses Busy Bee in a rhyme. The Force tries to rock with out there DJ and the Crash crew time on, unfortunately is finish before there act is done because somebody starts shooting. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 74-1st party - Cold Crush Four and Doug E. Fresh at the Shirley Arena in Long Island (1984)/ 2nd party - Doug E. Fresh, Crazy Eddie, Silver Fox at Manhattan Ville Projects (1982)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: K.G. starts off rocking to "Billie jean", they are mostly saying new rhymes. Caz does a rhyme about sexing a girl and about being an MC GOD. K.G. mic was having a problem. Doug E. Fresh opens up to "what people do for money". He battles His DJ Chill Will Human beat box battles the DJ He has a lot of fan participation.) 2nd party which is only a quarter of the tape has Doug E. Fresh and Crazy Eddie are rocking in Manhattan Ville projects and along came Silver Fox who did his thing. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 75-Doug E. Fresh, Tito of the Fearless four, Kid West, Magic Dee house tape

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I would have to say that Kid west rocked this tape then Tito and then Doug. Magic Dee also made his presence felt. Not a full 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 76-Grandmaster Flash, Kurtis Blow, Disco Four at Randys Place (1982, Side A)/ Treacherous Three, Spooney Dee, Moe Gee at unknown location (Side A)/ Johnny Wa, Rayvon, Kool Kyle, Treacherous Three at P.A.L. in Harlem (1981, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is three different party's on this one tape that are BLAZING. Side A: It sounds as if there is fight at the end cause the MC keeps saying please get off the tables and calm down. On that same side another party with an unknown location it's the Treacherous 3 along with Spoonie Gee and Moe Dee makes reference to the up coming battle between them and Furious Five. It appears obvious that this battle was already negotiated, but as history shows it never happened. I asked L.A. once why did they never battle, he said the furious just kept on ducking them. Side B: Johnny Wa and Rayvon do a fantastic show it almost sounds like Rayvon is doing DJ Hollywood's style. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 77-Funky 4+1 and the Zulu Nation at 123 park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There is about to be two different fights at this party. Africa Islam and Jazzy Jay are cutting and it is Breakouts birthday. They also rock Flash is on the beat box plate. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 78-Funky 4+1 at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The crew is doing work, but Rodney Cee steals the show doing rhymes about his sons a girl name melody and smoking dust and other rhymes. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 79-Lisa Lee, Rahiem, Pow Wow at outside jam (undated)/ Funky 4+1 at T-Connection (1980)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lisa Lee kills it on this party like I never heard her it is worth listening to. The crew (Funky 4+1) is rocking off the echo chamber. Keith Keith does a rhyme with the gilligans island melody and Rodney cee does a rhyme about how the crew got together.60 mins.

did.unittitle: 80-Whipper Whip, Dota Rock, Frankie Rowe, Bill Blast, Jerry Dee, Warlock 5 at T-Connection (1980)/Wiz Kid, DJ Lady Love, Afrika Bambaataa, MC Globe at T-Connection (1981)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: They are mostly passing the mic around, the Warlock 5 show is not complete. Show #2: the DJs are just cutting a lot of records half are cool out records and it feels like every body is laid back. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 81-Fantastic Five, Touch of Class Disco at T-Connection (1980)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is mostly Touch of Class Disco party but only Apollo One and Terry Tee were able to perform. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 82-Brothers 4, Pernelleo, Pernelleo's little brother, Bam Boo Crew, Master Don and the Def Committee, Mafia Family, Fearless Four, Mr. Bond, DJ Mr. Freeze, Elmo, Dollar Bill at Terrace Ballroom

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a contest and one of the judges is L.A. Sunshine. I am not happy with this tape cause I cannot tell if this tape sounds scratchy or gravely cause of DJ equipment or cause of the systems that were recording this after the show. I would give the trophy to Master don and the crew. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 83-Zulu Kings: Grandmaster Caz, Mellie Mel, Busy Bee, Bronx Style Bob, Ice Tee, Donald Dee, Lil Steve and MC Star Lite at Prospect Theater and Zodiac Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Zulu Kings do there thing, especially when they do there routine about marijuana laced with crack, which are called woolers. They use the record called rumors by I believe the group timex social club. The show is pretty good. One thing that has not change they are insulting the hell out of hecklers. Ice Tee makes reference to the infamous Larry Davis being caught by the cops the night before and so Ice Tee tells the crowd he had his bail and the crowd loved it. Also there is a third of a show from the zodiac club on there, pretty much the same thing. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 84-Zulu 16th Anniversary-Lord Finesse, Jungle Brothers, B.D.P., KRS1, Doug E. Fresh and Jazzy Jay at Bronx River Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: KRS1 and Doug put on a hell of a show, Finesse and jungle put on a good show also. 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: More from this recording can be found on 130 (tr-19392)

did.unittitle: 85-Kid Capri, Star Child, Busy Bee, Cowboy at the Roof Top

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cowboy in the house for one side doing his thing. a very interestingly funny tape. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 86-Brucie B., Busy Bee at the Roof Top

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Busy Bee is a very funny brother here and the party is rockin. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 87-Kid Capri at the Roof Top

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 88-Love Bug Starski at the Roof Top

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There are thousands of these tapes. I remember it was 86 or 87 my man Big Bub said this brother Kid Capri of the Roof Top uptown 155st was selling tapes of the party's he was DJing, for $10 and right away all the fellas were jumping on it and the rest is history. Cause to me that's were all these mix tapes of today started from, Kid Capri and Brucie Bee as well as Kool Kyle the Star Child. These tapes were the thing back then. Although Capri was banging I liked Brucie Bee better I guest cause he had the chill voice with the lil echo. Yo the best thing I can tell you, if you are interested in more of these type of tapes, Check my man Rock of "Rock and Will" his store is at 23west 125th st. in back of the jewelry counter. between Lenox and 5th or call him at 1-212-534-2056. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 89-Grandmaster Caz, K.G., Donald Dee, Ikey Cee and the Force MCs with Jazzy Jay cutting for them at Bronx River Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caz and K.G. cut off too soon, and Jazzy Jay is cutting real good for the Force MCs, especially when he cuts "catch the beat". Ikey Cee is also DJing on this tape. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 90-Kid Vicious, Donald Dee, Soulsonic Force, Afrika Islam, DJ Superman at the Renaissance at Bronx River Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: It starts to Mardi gras and they are using the echo chamber. Master Ice does a rhyme about "chuck a luck, black jack, five card charlie and cee low. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 91-Mr. Hyde, Mr. Bond, Star Child, DJ Ronnie Green, DJ Artie Art at the Foster Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DJ Artie Art is doing some great DJing as well as Ronnie Green. I am not sure that's the same Kool Kyle the Star Child, if it is he might not have reached puberty yet. He does rhymes pertaining to cartoon characters. Other then that they are playing some beautiful records such as Toys in the attic, Groove to get down, dance to drummers beat and others and they are using an echo chamber.

did.unittitle: 92-Harlem World crew- Son of Sam, Charlie Rock, DJ Randy, DJ Tastic at outside Jam in the area of The Valley and Section 5 of the Bronx

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First the Harlem World crew come on and they do there thing, I like there style I hope to get more tapes of them. The Mean Machine (Butch Kid, Julio, Slick Rick and Jimmy Mac) come on last and they get booed terribly and told collectively by the crowd to go home even after Jimmy Mac does a rendition of Michael Jackson's song. In between these two groups is a contest between such groups as the Ultimate 4, Famous MCs, Casanova 4 and the Hypnotizing 5. The Famous MCs won. Also Rasta Kid got on the mic for a minute.

did.unittitle: 93-Cold Crush Four- Record release party at Danceteria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cold Crush release there record "The Heartbreakers". In this show L.A. Starts the Four off the record by Prince "Lets go crazy" and they start to rock there records "weekend", "Punk rock rap" which J.D.L. rocks, "the Heartbreakers" and "Fresh, Fly, Wild and Bold and do some of there routines. The whole show is not recorded but at least 45 minutes is in. I fill it up with a show with J.D.L., K.G. and A.D. at another location.60 mins.

did.unittitle: 94-Tito and Paso of the Fearless 4 at a house party (undated, Side A)/Devastating 3, DJ Barry Bee at Barry's mother house (July 5th, 1983, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: This is a house joint and the DJ is Grandmaster C.O.P. They start rocking to the actual record they used to "Rocking It" They do a routine to the record "Dog a donut". DJ Charlie Rock comes on for a minute. Tito does a rhyme getting stuck up and Paso does one about sexing his teacher they rhyme together on meeting the president Jimmy Carter and rocking at the white house.) Side B : This is supposedly there last time rocking together, Kid West is going off to college. Doug Fresh comes on the very beginning for like an intro then the 3 do a routine from earth wind and fire and others and free style. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 95-Whiz Kid, Ramsey Rams, DJ Elroy, MC Spanky, Lil Dave at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a Kool Herc production. Whiz Kid fresh coming off winning a DJ battle a week earlier, passes on to another DJ who thinks he can do better, and displays that he has talent. Kool Herc speaks for a minute but briefly. Also announce a month from that day Whiz Kid will have a record release. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 96-Grandmaster Flash, Furious Five, Jazzy J, Afrika Bambaataa at T-Connection (undated, Side A)/Jazzy Five in New Jersey (1981, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: This maybe two different party's but it opens with jazzy j, and Bambaataa and they rock the Flash on the beat box plate. Next Flash and them come on and they are very involve and rock the record "walk like sex", hut and others and flash slices in that Flash Gordon piece. Not a full side only about 20minutes. Side B: Start off doing a routine off of Jackson 5 melody "never can say goodbye". Mr. Freeze does a rhyme about "the game of life" and they rock "Jazzy Sensation".60min

did.unittitle: 97-DJ Grandmaster Flowers at outside jam in Brooklyn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is the legendary DJ Flowers and you can definitely hear why. Although it is all disco music you can tell why he is categorized with the other legends. His MC, well I don't know. His name is Barren. Flowers runs into some tech problems as well he uses sound effects. 120 mins.

did.unittitle: 98-DJ Grandmaster Flowers at St. Marks Park, Brooklyn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flowers has a couple MCs Greg Cee, Derrick Bee, MC Lee Wild Mike E and John V and DJ Blue. Its mostly disco but he adds in a few breaks such as Super sperm and they rock the echo. 120min.

did.unittitle: 99-Zulu Nation 6th Anniversary (pt.1) Soulsonic Force, Jazzy Jay at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Soul Sonic get busy and it is nice and clear. Check tape 30 for the 2nd part. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 100-Troy L.'s ALL STARS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This tape will have studio recordings from MCs and groups from 1977 to 1983 as well as "LIVE" pieces of shows from that era.

did.unittitle: 101-Cold Crush Four, Salt and Pepper MCs at South Bronx High School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Whipper Whip and Dotta- Rock are rocking right with the Cold Crush passing the mic back and forth, and they are rocking the echo chamber real thick. K.G. does a rhyme about different soda's, Dot does one about fly girls off the record Toys in the attic. This is a prom or graduation party for the South Bronx High School. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 102-Funky4+1 more at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Funky do a routine accapella off of the melody "I know I am in love" by Chi Chi and Pepe. They do the record "That's the joint" for the first time. Rodney Cee calls Breakout "break a ball".

did.unittitle: 103-Jazzy Five at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I am doing a big check on this crew, cause this is not the Jazzy FIve that did " Jazzy Sensation" with Jazzy J and Mr. Freeze and the crew. This crew consist of Debbie Dee, Jazzy Ace, Busy E, Sinister Rock and Darrell Dee and the DJs are Master Plan Randy Ran and Patty Duke. Also it is Dot-A-Rocks birthday and Wiz Kid is also cutting. 60 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recordings related to this can be found on Tape 66 (tr-19328), Tape 133 (tr-19395), and Tape 294 (tr-19556)

did.unittitle: 104-Zulu Nation- Red Alert, Master Dee, Master Bee, Jazzy Jay, Globe and Pow Wow at unknown location in Brentwood, Long Island

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Master Bee does a rhyme about shooting dice and another about giving directions to girl on how to get to the T-Connection. Master Dee does a rhyme about getting busy with a girl and another about a fool and hard times. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 105-Force MCs at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From south side of Queens and the members are Cool Dee, Jazzy Gee, Sha-heam, Pookie, Darrell Dee, Disco Gee and bust these names Rahiem, Starsky and there DJs name is Busy Bee. Somebody give me some history on this crew. Other performers were Keith Keith of the Herculoids, Easy Gee and Jazzy Jay T-Connection. Note: It is Kool Herc and Bam's birthday.

did.unittitle: 106-Zulu Nation- Cosmic Force, Pow Wow, Globe, Mr. Bigg Jazzy J, Hutch Hutch, Donald Dee at Audubon Ballroom and in Long Island

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is 3 different party's. 1st. Audubon Pow Wow rocks a rhyme about a bandit from the western days stealing and hiding money and coming back 10 years later, after spending his money on women and a good time he later on goes to the desert and commits suicide. And the Cosmic force kill it with there routine. 2nd party in Long Island Hutch Hutch does a rhyme about the incredible Hulk having funky cloths and dingy hair. Pow Wow does a rhyme about buffalo bill. 3rd party Jay is known as Lil Jazzy Jay or this is somebody who is Jays understudy, Pow Wow does a rhyme about Lisa Lee calling him up and telling him to come over and rock. Donald Dee does a rhyme about meeting a girl name Tiny, Hutch Hutch does a rhyme about his daughter. They are using the echo chamber. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 107-Zulu Nation- Sundance, Jazzy Jay, Hutch Hutch, Pow Wow at 123 Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sundance does a rhyme about crooked things, teasing a girl in the bed before banging her and another about the channels on T.V. Hutch Hutch does a rhyme about meeting a girl on the train and another about bring two girls around. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 108-Rock steady, Kevie Kev, Whipper Whip, DJ Theodore at Danceteria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a party that the Rock steady dancers are doing there thing, even got a brother with one leg from New Jersey doing his thing. Also this is during the time Kevie Kev is about to start rockin with Flash and the Furious 5 so you hear him saying some of there rhymes. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 109-Caz, Busy Bee, Furious Five, Zulu Nation at Audubon Ballroom and T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mellie Mel on the echo, they play there album Freedom for the first time but they are having mic problems so they turn the record over. Pow Wow does a rhyme about how he got life in jail, Master Bee does a rhyme about dice, Master Ice does one about how he got to Rikers Island, Lisa Lee does one about Jazzy Jay rockin the music when she got off the train also does the "chittie chittie bang bang" rhyme. Busy Bee is rockin with the record "it's the joint" and him and Caz are clowning on the mic about who is in the party. Then they go back and forth with there rhymes. Mandip lite and Herc are at the party.

did.unittitle: 110-Tito and O.G. Rock at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sounds like a house tape and O.C. is the DJ records like head hunters, catch the beat genius of love and dog the donut among others that are being played. Tito announces that "its magic will be out shortly and they will be doing it off dog the donut" O.G. does a rhyme about Kennedy high school and a rhyme about the King Queen and jack among others. Tito does rhymes by D.L.B. and Moe Dee as well as how they got together and a rhyme about D.L.B. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 111-Fantastic Five, Rockin Rob and the MC Connection Crew at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There is an award ceremony. Busy Bee and Lil Spank of the Inner City crew, give an award for Flash as the best DJ and the Furious 5 as the Best group and most promising. Funky 4 got 2nd place. Apache, Barry Bee, Lil Rich, Lil Bit and Crazy ? make up the MC Connection. Kevie Kev sings along with the records of "Just a touch of Love" and "Rock with you" and Theodore once again shows why he was so great at that time by cutting "Rock with you" so well. And Fantastic Start there Hey Fellas routine. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 112-Zulu Nation at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a true Zulu Nation throw down. This is 2 party's and both of them kill it. 1st. party is at the T-Connection Jazzy J is the DJ, Smitty Dee is on the echo, Kid Vicious, Tricky Tee, Malibu, Pow Wow and Nae Nae are the MCs Malibu uses the same melody but changes some of the words to one of Caz's rhymes, which Caz also borrowed a couple verses from Johnny Cash, that's right the country singer. It's Troy Troy's birthday. 2nd party Bambaataa is the DJ and this is were he plays a lot of his fine collection, Dance to the drummers, you are what you are, keep your distance, groove to get down, "fuck you" by Millie Jackson, love and affection and Jamaican or African song were the singers lyrics are about the t.v. mini series "Roots". Also a Latin jam were the singer is saying specific people are "a wanted man". And it was Rodney Cee's birthday. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 113-Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five at Lace's Roller Rink in Roosevelt, Long Island

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Only one mic is working so the mic is being passed around amongst the Furious 5. Kurtis Blow's record "that's the breaks" must of just come out cause they played it a few times. Flash is using the Infinity machine's equipment.

did.unittitle: 114-Mighty Mike Cee, Rob Bass, Doug Fresh, MC Holiday, Magic Dee, Kid West, Jay Z, MC Ronnie Dee, DJ Barry Bee at Lincoln Projects in Harlem (1984, Side A)/ Tito, Mike Cee and Peso. DJ Crazy Eddie and DJ Reggie Reg at Manhattan Ville Projects in Harlem (undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: Mike Cee is doing work saying rhymes from his records and freestyling. Magic Dee kills it with his "Charlie, Molly, Wally, Polly rhyme. MC Holiday insults Rob Bass in a battle Rhyme, Rob Bass takes the high road and hits him back with a good quality rhyme, Holiday comes back with an average rhyme and then backs out of the battle. Doug does some human beat box for the devastating 3 MCs as well as his own rhymes. This tape starts out grainy but comes out decent. Side B: D.L.B. is not there but Fearless still kills it. UTFO is in the crowd. Crazy Eddie is cutting together the whistle from Spoonin Rap and the beat from Love Rap together, sounds real good.

did.unittitle: 115-L-Brothers (circa 1978, Side A)/Fantastic Five (1980, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Master Rob calls him self Robbie Rob. Busy Bee is a different MC while with the L-Brothers. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 116-The World Famous Supreme Team Show

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Supreme Team show was ran by C Divine the Master Mind and Just Allah the Superstar and on this show The Force MCs are in the house doing there thing and some brothers from Jersey City known as Sweet Slick and Sly are in the studio doing there thing and the shows just bring back some memories. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 117-The World Famous Supreme Team Show

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Just Allah the Superstar does his routine called "Enlighten." I for got they also played Soul songs from the seventies on there shows and there doing it here. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 118-The World Famous Supreme Team Show

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Force MCs are representing again. My projects the Grant Projects is given a shout out, and at that time Grant was called GODS PROJECTS by the 5 Percenters, and they had very strong following in there at that time. Also The Earth Neffertiti of GODS PROJECTS gives a shout out of love to her GOD L-Sun. Some other fool is giving a shout out, is cut off just before he says something very embarrassing. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 119-The Battle of the DJs at The New Music Seminar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The DJs that battle are Charlie Chase, Jazzy J and Whiz Kid. Each DJ had an outstanding performance. It is not easy at all for me to pick who won. The Cold Crush Four performed for 5 minutes at the end. Grandmaster Caz is one of the host. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 120-Cold Crush Four 21st Anniversary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I believe it is 1997. A DJ Wendell starts out for this show. Then the CC4 come on and do there routine "other MCs". J.D.L. is not in attendance. After the routine, Caz talks about the difference between MCs and rappers. Then the crew goes into a small routine about this subject. Crash crew come on next. La Shue Bee, Darrell Cee, Reggie Reg and E.K. Mike C are the only ones in attendance for the Crash. Next up Jazzy J is on the 1's and 2's mixing a Erica Badu cut while Tricky Tee, Rodney Cee and Jazzy Jeff come on one by one. I take my hat off to Jazzy Jeff he did his thing. Rahiem comes on and he does his thing starting out in French. Jazzy Joyce later comes on. Caz comes on again does his thing taking his rhymes from back in the day and still having them sound dope today. Also Doug Fresh comes on and rocks the human beat box at the same time while Caz does his thing. This is also during the death of Biggie Smalls and Tupac. So Doug is asking for peace. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 121-Kurtis Blow at The Apollo (1985, Side A)/Africa Bambaataa and the Soul Sonic Force at the Fun House (1984, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: This show starts off with DJA.J. battling Davy DMX it only last for about 5 min. DMX cuts a lil faster then A.J. who is known as "A.J. Scratch" now. But then Grandmaster Flash comes up and after his set, he says "they are two very good DJ's but know one is faster then Grandmaster Flash". Then Kurtis Blow goes into his records Christmas Rap, the Breaks and others. Timmy Tee of Hollis Queens comes and does the human beat box for Kuris Blow as he sings daydreaming. Side B: Jelly Bean maybe the host. Bam is on the beat box and the crowd is amp cause the Soul Sonic Force are about to do there record Planet Rock. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 122-Grand Wizzard Theodore and Master Rob at birthday party at Ecstasy Garage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cold Crush Four, Rodney Cee and Keith Keith perform for Rob and Theodore and Fantastic do there thing. Frank Nitti and China get shout outs. Also Mean Gene is on the One's and two's for a minute. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 123-Kevie Kev and Ruby Dee at birthday party at Ecstasy Garage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush Four and Busy Bee participate in the party. Dota- Rock cousin Presweet is in the house. Dot does his rhyme about an Amazon. J.D.L. is singing along to heart break hotel by Michael Jackson. 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Remainder of recording can be found on tape 153 (tr-19415) and tape 157 (tr-19419)

did.unittitle: 124-Cold Crush Four and Treacherous Three on boat ride to Bear Mountain (1982, Side A)/Busy Bee at Harlem World (Side B)/Force MCs at Valley Stream (1983, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 party's on this tape. Side A: Boat ride. I finally found it! This is where Caz does both rhymes of him dissing a heckler. Caz also does a rhyme about a pick pocket. Caz also say's some chick was from the "ity bitty titty committee". L.A. Sunshine does a rhyme about a fat lady. L.A. also talks about the Moe Dee Busy Bee battle. There's hissing. 2nd show on side A is Busy Bee at Harlem World. Side B: This is the ending to the Treacherous 3 first anniversary. Busy is rocking off of "Heart beat". There's hissing. Force MCs at Valley Stream 1983. It sounds like a battle but they never say who they are going against. The Lord K-Whan is rockin with Stevie Dee and Mercury. Dr. Rock displays his speed on the turntables. K-Whan does a rhyme about Ronald Reagan. They do the Underdog routine off of Mary Mary. They let anyone from the crowd come up to do the electric boogie. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 125-Busy Bee, Boogie Boys, DJ Spivey, DJ A.J. at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Busy Bee and Sha-Rock birthday. Spivey of the Magnificent 7 is cutting real good. One of records he is cutting is Cat Stevens "Dog a Donut". A.J. only comes on for a minute. Boogie Boys and Busy Bee are having a lot of fun rockin together. Sha Rock never comes on. Busy Bee does a rhyme were he names all the Solo MCs (such as Spoonie, Hollywood and anybody else from that time) and says "But there's none like me". In the beginning of the show someone in the crowd continued to flash a flash light in Bee's face. Irritating him. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 126-L-Brothers: Busy Bee, Kevie Kev, Master Rob (also called Robbie Rob), Theodore, Mean Gene, Claudio at unknown location (1978, Side A)/Notorious 2, DJ Dr. Pepper and MC DJ Whitehead at Creston Ave block party (1979, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Echo is being used. Show starts out with Groove to get down. Later on Trans Europe Express is played all the way out. Side B: This is a block party on Creston ave. Caz says he wants to kiss the ground because he remembers back when some one wanted to try and stab him right on that block. He also makes reference to Big Bank Hank and says the rhymes that he gave Hank. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 127-Cosmic Force Jazzy Jay, Mr. Biggs and Hutch Hutch (1980, Side A)/Zulu Nation jam - Bug Out and Ice Ice birthday at T-Connection (1980, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: They just sounded like they were having a good time. Side B: starts out with Bam playing Flash beat box plate and the MCs doing mic checks. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 128-DJ Stevie Steve, Lil Rodney Cee and Lil Shot Gun at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rodney Cee is with his first DJ Stevie Steve, before Rodney got down with Funky 4+1more. Many records are being played that I never heard. But one I recognized and have been looking for is the Smokin cheeba cheeba by Harlem Underground by the brother George Benson. As well as the Sing sing sing by Incredible Bongo Band. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 129-Fearless Four at Harlem World (1982, Side A)/Grandmaster Caz birthday party at unknown location (1983, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is the Treacherous 3 second Anniversary. The Fearless come on before Cold Crush and Treacherous 3. You can connect this with tape number 6. Fearless do routines and there record It's Magic. D.L.B has a nice solo when says a rhyme about him Moe and L.A. growing up where they lived at playing the 52 punches game. The Disco 4 is in the house. Side B: Party starts off to Billie Jean. Also play sucker MCs, Beat it and Candy girl among other records. They announce there record the Heartbreakers is about to be released. Kid Kreole announces that he is supposed to battle Busy Bee. Joey Kane photographer of the Cold Crush is announced. Cold Shocking 5 perform. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 130-Zulu Nation 16th Anniversary at Bronx River Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This show is connected to tape 84. Pow Wow, Mele Mel (Mel really puts his Mack down) Queen mother Rage, Y.Sell and Daddy Rich, Lord Finesse, Yam Boo, MC Philosopher and Mecca perform among others. 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: More from this recording can be found on tape 84 (tr-19346)

did.unittitle: 131-Disco 4, Fearless 4, Master Don and the Def Committee, Spoonie Gee, MC Maniac, MC Playboy at Manhattan Ville Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wiz Kid is now the DJ for Funky 4+1 more. Disco 4 free styles. Mr. Troy does his cutting up frogs rhyme. K.G. speaks on rumor that the Cold Crush is not breaking up. D.L.B. talks about how Rockin it is no.1 on W.B.L.S radio station. Master Don is rocking records and the Beatbox. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 132-Live Convention - Grandmaster Flash, Disco Bee, Busy Bee, Melle Mel and E- Man at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Check tape 57. This is the 2nd part to the Live Convention tape. This E- Man is from the Bronx! Not from the Magnificent 7 but his style is like E-Man and Rayvon from Magnificent 7 of Manhattan. E-Man sings off of "Funk you right up". Mele Mel's Fiancé is in the house.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First half of recording can be found on tape 57 (tr-19319)

did.unittitle: 133-Dota Rock's Birthday party - Fantastic Five, Jazzy Five at unknown location (1980, Side A)/Kevie Kev and Rubie Dee birthday party at Ecstasy Garage (1980, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: This is more of tape 66. It's Sandwich birthday also. Dot does his like routine. Side B: The rest of tape 123. Busy Bee and the Cold Crush rock J.D.L. mentions the wine is getting him high. Busy Bee comments that they have a slide show for the birthday boys. 90 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recordings related to this can be found on Tape 66 (tr-19328), Tape 103 (tr-19365), and Tape 294 (tr-19556)

did.unittitle: 134-Treacherous Three and Spoonie G at outside jam in Connecticut (Undated, Side A)/Summer Youth Core Check Day with Jazzy Five, Ez Trick, Incredible Four, Cosmic Force, Lightning Lance at outside jam (Undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a outside jam. L.A. Sunshine is not in attendance this night. Disco Kid and MC Divine of Infinity Machine perform as well as Lady Love DJ's. Spoonie free styles as well as perform one of his records. Side B another outside jam. It is summer youth core check day. The crowd just doesn't know how to act on check day. Jazzy FIve are trying to do a free show. It's Master Bee birthday. Ez Trick is wildin out. Another show comes on with the Incredible 4 and Cosmic Force. Also Lightning Lance puts his mack down. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 135-MC Battle at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is like some amateur MC battle with a few ringers. The crew Phase 3 from Queens, MC Don Juan DJ Cut Master. Another MC that was real good but I never heard his name. His voice sounded like he was from Europe and his routine was real good in fact he should have won the contest. Towards the end of his routine he sounded like Howard Cousell and Rona Barret. Cool A.J. Rapper Wise, Ultimate 3 MCs, DJ Disco Quick. Sweet Slick and Sly, D.st and his MCs using a synthesizer. 60 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The recording that precedes the performances on this tape can be found on tape 275 (tr-19537)

did.unittitle: 136-Dr. Jeckle Mr. Hyde, Funky 4+1 at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ronnie Green is the DJ for Jeckle and Hyde. Some one makes an announcement "if you got the dust come up front".

did.unittitle: 137-All Zulu throw down at Monroe High School (Undated, Side A)/Whiz Kid, Globe, Pow Wow and Mr. Biggs at T-Connection (1981, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: The Usual Suspects; Mr. Big, Pow Wow, Hutch Hutch, Lisa Lee make up Soul Sonic Force. Jazzy Jay and Sundance. Dynamite Brothers are Ikea Cee and Ice Ice who sounds very young as they do a routine using the alphabet. Also Rocky Dee and Chubby Chub. The Echo is used. Pow Wow does a rhyme about Pierre and Timmy Tim going to jail and getting the electric chair.) Side B: Zulu have an electric boogie contest (Which was called bionic boogie contest at that time.) $50 to the winner. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 138-Devastating 3 MCs with Kid West, Magic Dee and Barry Bee at unknown location (circa 1983, Side A)/Doug E. Fresh, Barry Bee, Chuck a Luck, Sweet Slick and Sly and the Tranquilizing 3 at unknown location (undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: They do a routine with the lean on me theme, also Lavern and Shirley theme. Rock records like juicy fruit, I like it, and between sheets. Sounds like 1983 House tape. Side B: the Tranquilizing 3 do a Lavern and Shirley routine. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 139-Super Sonic 4 MCs at Ecstasy Garage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consist of Ricky Dee, Vicious Cee, Dwayne Dee, Ice Ice and Lil Sha Rock. DJ Cisco and Cool Clyde. Ice Ice putting his mack down. Vicious does a rhyme about biting no more. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 140-DJ A.J. Birthday at Ecstasy Garage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fantastic Five Johnny Wa and Rayvon and Busy Bee. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 141-Grandmaster Flash and The Furious Five at unknown location in Helsinki, Finland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flash opens up with Wheels of steel. Larry Love is introduced to the Finland crowd as the newest member of the crew. The people in Finland really love them. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 142-MC Convention at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fantastic Five, Pow Wow and Globe. Cold Crush Four, Cosmic Force, A.J. and Busy Bee. Side B Jazzy FIve with Wanda Dee. Everybody does short sets, it sounds like a competition, but their was no winner declared. Note: somebody told Globe jokingly to get off the stage after he finished his rhyme. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 143-Master Don and the Def Committee at Rucker Park, 155th St., 8th Avenue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tito and Mike Cee of the Fearless 4 came in the park and rocked with the Committee. This was a battle with a crew called the Romantic 5 MCs There part is not on the tape. Everybody rocked. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 144-Marauder's Fury at unknown location (undated, Side A)/ Sweet, Slick and Sly, Tito and Doug E. Fresh at a house (undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: Marauder's Fury is group that Gangster Gee made up after he left Master Don and the Def Committee. Side B: Tito and Doug are rocking with Sweet Slick and Sly at what sounds like a House tape. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 145-L-Brothers, Kevie Kev, Master Rob, Busy Bee and Theodore at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kev says the way Theodore is back spinning it sounded better than a beat box, he told the crowd it was the new beat box. Kev says if it wasn't so cold we would tear the roof off the place. The spank dance was out at this time. Theodore played a lot of records that I had not heard before. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 146-Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five at Edeamore Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Starts off with Flash playing Funky Drummer, Mardi Gras and Johnny the Fox. Cowboy tells the MCs to raise there mics. Mr. Ness does a nice rhyme about growing up in the Bronx. Mel tells the people from the Bleachers to look at what Flash was doing on the turntables. Flash, Easy Mike, and Disco Bee, all cutting Bounce Rock Skate together. I don't know if Mel slipped but he called Creole by his original name Benny Ben. The crowd was really in it at times. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 147-Larry Levan at Ecstasy Garage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DJ Larry Levan is putting in work, with a lot of disco and R&B music. He also uses sound effects. I have let other people hear this tape and they really like it. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 148-The Original Funky 4 at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sha Rock, Keith Keith, K.K. Rockwell and Rahiem. DJs Breakout and Baron. This is during the time when Rahiem was a member of the Funky Four. Unfortunately they are not doing any routines. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 149-Cold Crush at S.O.B.s. (1993, Side A)/Zulu Nation 14th Anniversary at unknown location (circa 1988, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: Money Ray steps in for J.D.L. The time is between Christmas and New Years. Caz says you can only make the telephone once, the Cold Crush are the Alexander Graham Bells of MCs Caz also says that Just Ice was the first gangster rapper, Easy E and West coast came later. The Cold Crush do a few totally new routines, one is called "One Nigger, two nigger, three nigger, four." Side B: Ikey Cee, Mele Mel, Kid Dust, Jungle Brothers, Doug E. Fresh and Barry Bee. Barry and Doug battle each other in a very good show. Barry Bee kills it cutting Funky Drummer. Absolutely kills it, it is a must hear. Jungle Brothers also rock, and they some funny brothers. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 150-The Original Funky 4 at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is the Original Funky 4. The tape is not of good quality, but this is a hell of good find. All those years I always wondered why they were so revered; now I know why. I am still in search for a 3 MCs tape aka the Furious 3, to see who the best was at that time. The reason why I say that is, the Funky 4 with Rahiem is unbelievable. The L-Brothers just past the mic around on all the tapes I heard of them so far, The Original Funky 4 put on a hell of a show. They are doing the Crazy Eddie routine, Plop plop, fizz fizz routine and others, and Rahiem is singing. They also are rocking records like Paradise and others. 78 mins.

did.unittitle: 151-Doug E. Fresh and Barry Bee, Devastating 3, Tantalizing 3 at unknown locations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: It is two different shows. Doug rocks his High Bridge rhyme. Doug and Barry go head up. Doug does beat box. Tantalizing 3 does a Mary Tyler Moore routine. Chuck a Luck has a real nice style. His partners are Al Bal and Doug Sexxx. Rasta is rocking too. Devastating 3 kill it on the other side. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 152-Zulu Nation at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DJ Red Alert, Pow Wow, Mr. Big, Randy Ran, Easy G, Tony Tee does a rhyme about Boys and Girls. Master Ice is singing to the record "Watching you." 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 153-Fantastic Freaks at unknown location (circa 1983, Side A)/Kevie Kev and Ruby Dee's birthday at Ecstasy Garage (1980, Side A)/Cold Crush Four, Valentine's Day at Ecstasy Garage (undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Their is no year for Fantastic Freaks I assume it is 1983. Dota Rock is called Ice Man. Another is called Heartbreak Kid, and another is called Celebrity Freak. They do a couple of new routines one is about Elmira Prison. The second part of side a is another part of Kevie Kev and Ruby Dee's birthday. Side B is Valentines day at the Estacy Garage and the Cold Crush are rocking. 60 mins.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Remainder of birthday party recording (sdie A) can be found on tape 123 (tr-19385) and tape 157 (tr-19419)

did.unittitle: 154-Fantastic Five and Rayvon at Celebrity Club (undated, Side A)/Zulu Nation, Jazzy Five at unknown location in Mount Vernon (undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fantastic Five at Celebrity Club 1980 Easter Sunday. They are only rocking with two mics. Kev says in his rhyme "I don't play the game of the Rappers Delight because originality is what I like." Rayvon is rocking with DJ Spivey. Side b Zulu is up in Mount Vernon. Jazzy Jay is on the turntables. Pow Wow and the crew is joking with the crowd having a good time, even stating they will throw money into the crowd, Pow Wow said he had a hundred pennies for them. Later Jazzy FIve rock the crowd. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 155-Hollywood at unknown location (1986, Side A)/Doug E. Fresh and Eric B and Rakim at the Roof Top (1987, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hollywood rocking in 1986. Doing his "Well" routine and "Poo Tang" routine. His man Capital L gets on the mic. Hollywood also DJs and mixes a whole lot of records. Unknown location. Side b. Doug E. Fresh, Rakim and Eric B are rocking at the Roof Top in 1987 and it is Doug's Birthday. L.L. Cool J and others are at his party. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 156-Kool Moe Dee, Dana Dane and Heavy Dee at the Roof Top

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Heavy Dee is on last, he must have been the hottest at the time. Moe freestyles before he goes into his "Go see the Dr." Dana Dane sounds real good even though he forgets some of his record. Heavy Dee puts on a good show also. Host of the show is very disrespectful. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 157-Treacherous Three with DJ Darrell Cee at unknown location (undated, Side A)/Kevie Kev, Rubie Dee party at Ecstasy Garage (1980, Side A)/Force MCs at Broadway International (undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Treacherous 3 kills it for twenty minutes. I don't know where they are at, but there is no routines none of their albums just freestyle, and they are killing it. The other 10 minutes that is left is the rest of the Kevie Kev, Rubie Dee party, at the Ecstasy Garage. Busy Bee, A.J., J.D.L. do their thing. Side B: The Force MCs sing a melody from one of the Earth, Wind, and Fire songs. As well as do their famous routines. Stevie Dee is snapping on people again in the crowd. He might have pissed some body off because he is apologizing on this one. Dr. Shock is playing for the first time at Broadway International. B. Fats is cutting later on and doing his thing along with Ronnie Dee MCing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Remainder of birthday party recording (side A) can be found on tape 123 (tr-19385) and tape 153 (tr-19415)

did.unittitle: 158-Zulu Affair - T.C. Islam, AG, Roc Raider, Ikey Cee, Messengers of Funk, Doug E. Fresh, Fat Joe Ron Gee at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arthur X is the host. Ikey Cee is doing a Butter Parkay routine. Fat Joe having technical problems Doug Fresh and Ron Gee rock the beat box. The crowd really loves Doug. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 159-DJ Hollywood, Starski and DJ Smalls at Club 371 (1979, Side A)/Sweet Gee and June Bug at the Hotel Diplomat (Easter Sunday, 1981, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hollywood is rocking and he has his chorus of brothers rocking with him and they are called The Corporation. Their names are Captain Jack, Spanish Mack, L.T.D., June Bug Disco Romeo and Junior. DJ Smalls lets the crowd know that he is the son of Hollywood. From Hollywood I heard some rhymes that Busy Bee used later in 1981. Hollywood has a lot of rhymes, his choicer of records and rhythm is very good. The last ten minutes of side two has Sweet Gee on the mic and June Bug is the DJ at the Hotel Diplomat, on Easter Sunday 1981. Sweet Gee's Heart Beat record is out at this time. His performance blew my mind because he was doing Rayvon's rhymes of Johnny Wa and Rayvon. He killed it. he was very clear and he had his own rhymes but I couldn't believe that he would use so much of Rayvon's rhymes. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 160-Kurtis Blow, Jeckle and Hyde, Starski, Playgirls, Whodini at unknown location in Queens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is at a space in Queens and it is Supreme and Corley's birthday party. The crowd went crazy when Kurtis Blow did "A.J. scratch." Star Ski did "At the Fever" Dana Dane and U.T.F.O. get on the mic for a minute as Red Alert spins. Fat Cat and Russell Simmons are in the house. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 161-LL Cool J, Deuces Wild, Funk Master Flex, Omar Chandler, Justice Ice at unknown location in Southside Queens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is Chuck Chill out birthday party. Bizmark is on the side lines asking to get on next. B.E.T.s Rap City is in the house. Cool Chip, Dr. Ice, MC Delight and Dana Dane is in the house. Ralph McDaniel's is also in the house and he gets on the mic for a little while. L. L. is very sexual in his lyrics as he freestyles and does a couple of his records. Chandler sings his Joy and Pain, and other songs. Side B. Justice does Latoya the uncut version. Also does "Put that record back, The girl is a slut". DMX does his part. Hank Love is in the house. Two emcee's name Spank G and Dota Rock, with dancers name Prince God and Kool Raheim. They do a routine called "Do that S---." 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 162-Whodini, Jeckle and Hyde, Galaxy, Oran Juice Jones, Playgirls, Lady Blue, DJ Divine and the Infinity Machine at the Encore Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kango Kid and Dr. Ice get on with Whodini for a minute. They rock Friends, 5 Minutes of Funk, and Haunted Mansion. The Playgirls with Sparky Dee, Moe Ski and City Slime do their record but kill with their live routine. Oran Juice Jones is mostly a host. Jeckle and Hyde do their a.m. p.m. the crowd really digs them. Then they do Pumpkins All Stars by them selves. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 163-Fantastic Freaks, Busy Bee, Cly and Tell Brothers, Super Lover Emcees at unknown location in Hempstead, Long Island

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Herbie Luv bug, Playboy Prince Charm, Romeo the American Gigelo and DJ quick Silver make up the Super Lovers emcee's. They rock off of Dog the Donut. Cly and Tell Brothers do a little borrowing from J.D.L. and Special K. DJ Divine helps Theodore with some tech problems that Kevie Kev didn't know about. Whipper Whip is not there. Ruby Dee rhymes in Spanish. A lot of Jamaica and South side Queens is in the house. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 164-Sweet G Birthday party with Rat Ski, DJ Kevin Kanyeah, Emcee SPUD at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rat Ski is of the infamous Rat and Monkey of Queens. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 165-Grandmaster Flash and The Furious Five at the Hotel Diplomat (undated, Side A)/Infinity Machine at unknown location in Connecticut (undated, Side B)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Furious 5 is at the Hotel Diplomat. Once again the Furious are passing the mic around but the sound dam good doing it. it is a Sunday night. they are using Infinity Machines equipment. It is a Christmas night. towards the end emcee Disco kid and DJ Divine get on. Side B: Infinity Machine is in Connecticut. Emcee SPUD does routine off of F- Troop and the Jefferson's. Emcee Sir Rap, Freaky Tee, Jazzy J get on. James "Jimmy Beat Box rocks the beat box." Smurph dance was out at the time. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 166-Hot 97 show - The Furious Five at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This show is dedicated to the late great Cowboy. They do a routine off the theme from the Intruders "Cowboys to Girls". Kid Kreole says in the routine Throw your hands up, Put your money down. Everybody lay flat on the ground. Mark the 45 King is on the decks. They do some records and freestyle.

did.unittitle: 167-Hot 97 show - Funk Master Flex show featuring Grandmaster Flash and The Furious Five at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A lot of free styling. They do the Message and Super Rappin to Funky Drummer. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 168-Star Child at the Love Nest Club at after hours spot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: It is a strong debate who went first with the invention of the mix tape, but Star Child is definitely a King down with the other two Kings, Brucie Bee and Kid Capri, as he rocks R and B with Hip Hop. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 169-Kid Capri - R&B Classic at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is the Kid Capri Classic that many people in Harlem were rocking for years. It seemed like he broke ten year records all over again. He also seemed to put a new shine on the Jackson Five records as well. A true Kid Capri Classic.

did.unittitle: 170-Kid Capri at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kid Capri rocks Hip Hop in 1986. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 171-Kid Capri at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kid Capri rocks Hip Hop in 1986. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 172-World Famous Brucie B. and Busy Bee at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brucie Bee opens up with the cool echo voice by saying he is rocking on the Cool Out Side tonight. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 173-DJ Hollywood at Club 371
174-DJ Hollywood and Reggie Wells at Club 371

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Starts off with Hollywood talking about sex. Reggie Wells is DJing. Captain Jack is in the house. Hollywood gets on to DJ Sexual Healing was the hottest record at that time. Its Star Childs birthday. Hollywood does the "Well" routine. Prince Lover A.C. rocks the mic.

did.unittitle: 175-Eddie Cheeba at Martin Luther King Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eddie Cheba is rocking with Easy G and his Cheba Crew. This is the after the boat ride party. He often refers to the people in the party as the C and C people, meaning the Cocaine and Champagne people. The popular dance at the time was the Patty Duke and Jack Benny. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 176-DJ Whiz Kid house tape

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: this is a house tape strickly rocking in 1981. Whiz Kid is showing why he use to cut for Kool Herc, and why he was beating a lot of the known DJs of that time. 90 mins.

did.unittitle: 177-Afrika Islam, Jazzy Jay, Red Alert, Cosmic Force, Soul Sonic Force, Kid Vicious at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: unbelievable piece of work. The DJs are stealing the show with the records they play. There isn't any routines just them passing the mic around but the DJs are playing records like the Telephone jam with out the ring. Others I never heard, and then there are records I haven't heard since 1982. A real good listen. They want to know if there is anybody in the house with Cheba, to come up front.

did.unittitle: 178-Cosmic Force, Sha-Rock and Lisa Lee, L-Brothers at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: this is the Zulu 6th anniversary. Lisa Lee takes charge of the group and starts singing before they go into there routine. Ikey Cee being the captain wasn't happy with the situation, but he fell right in line. The second part of this side a, had Lisa Lee and Sha- Rock passing the mic back and forth. Side B: the L-Brothers rocking with out Kevie Kev. Master Rob and Busy Bee do there thing. Busy gives props to the L- Sisters. Theodore is going off again. 60 mins.

did.unittitle: 179-Funky 4 + 1 more at unknown location

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sha- Rocks birthday. Funky does a routine off of Happy Days Theme song Barron is doing his thing on the turntables. 60 mins.
